title: galium maritimum subsp. luteum
intro: Galium maritimum subsp. luteum, also known as Yellow Sea Milkwort, is a subspecies of the Galium maritimum plant, a type of flowering plant native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its high concentration of alkaloids and flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Galium maritimum subsp. luteum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
